proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
sex_female == 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than sex_female == 0.
Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status categories (never, former, current); current smokers have the shortest PFS.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Adenocarcinoma histology has longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
stage_iv == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv == 0 patients.
has_brain_mets == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: OLS coefficient on age_years = 0.1699 months/year (p=0). Older patients have slightly longer PFS.
Mean PFS female=3.319 mo vs male=3.523 mo; difference (F-M) = -0.204 mo (p=7.7e-25). Females have shorter PFS, supporting the directional hypothesis.
ANOVA F=433.0, p=3.65e-187. Means: never=3.675, former=3.607, current=2.987. Current smokers clearly shortest PFS.
ECOG coefficient = -1.099 mo per unit (p=0). Each ECOG point removes ~1.1 months PFS — strongest single predictor.
Adeno - squamous PFS diff = 0.857 mo (p=0). Adenocarcinoma substantially longer.
Stage IV - non-IV PFS diff = -1.438 mo (p=0). Stage IV markedly worse.
Brain-mets vs none PFS diff = -0.921 mo (p=0). Brain mets ~1 mo shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation == 1 patients have different (likely longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation == 0 patients.
kras_g12c == 1 patients have different pfs_months than kras_g12c == 0 patients.
alk_fusion == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion == 0 patients (since untargeted ALK fusion is aggressive).
stk11_mutation == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation == 0 patients.
brca2_mutation == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation == 0 patients.
tmb_high == 1 patients have longer pfs_months than tmb_high == 0 patients.
Higher pdl1_tps is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ mean PFS=3.596 vs EGFR-=3.407; diff=+0.189 mo (p=6.48e-11). Small but significant longer PFS in EGFR+.
KRAS G12C+ mean PFS=4.114 vs negative=3.332; diff=+0.782 mo (p=7.36e-94). Notable benefit, hinting at sotorasib effect (treated patients in mutation-positive group).
ALK fusion+ mean PFS=2.661 vs neg=3.472; diff=-0.810 mo (p=5.61e-66). ALK fusion strongly associated with shorter PFS in this cohort (no targeted ALK inhibitor available).
STK11 mut diff = 0.0091 mo (p=0.741). NOT significant — main effect null.
BRCA2 mut diff = -0.119 mo (p=0.0312). Marginally significant shorter PFS in BRCA2+.
TMB high - low diff = -0.190 mo (p=1.04e-17). TMB high actually has shorter PFS — opposite of immunotherapy-era expectations.
PD-L1 TPS coefficient = 0.0667 (p=0.157). NOT significant — PD-L1 TPS not associated with PFS overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is positively associated with pfs_months (better nutritional status → longer PFS).
Higher ldh_u_l is negatively associated with pfs_months (LDH reflects tumor burden / hypoxia).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively associated with pfs_months (cachexia → shorter PFS).
Higher crp_mg_l is negatively associated with pfs_months (systemic inflammation → shorter PFS).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
Other routine labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) are each individually associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: albumin_g_dl: beta = 0.4411 (p=2.8e-109). Significant.
ldh_u_l: beta = -0.0005108 (p=3.55e-05). Significant.
weight_loss_pct_6mo: beta = -0.07329 (p=7.48e-179). Significant.
crp_mg_l: beta = 0.0003117 (p=0.776). NOT significant.
nlr: beta = 0.003897 (p=0.416). NOT significant.
hemoglobin_g_dl: beta = -0.001805 (p=0.746). NOT significant.
alkaline_phosphatase_u_l: beta = -0.0002951 (p=0.144). NOT significant.
ast_u_l: beta = 0.0003265 (p=0.702). NOT significant.
alt_u_l: beta = -0.001054 (p=0.199). NOT significant.
total_bilirubin_mg_dl: beta = -0.02625 (p=0.406). NOT significant.
creatinine_mg_dl: beta = -0.04135 (p=0.218). NOT significant.
bun_mg_dl: beta = -0.001308 (p=0.514). NOT significant.
sodium_meq_l: beta = 0.002358 (p=0.478). NOT significant.
potassium_meq_l: beta = 0.0193 (p=0.332). NOT significant.
calcium_mg_dl: beta = -0.02112 (p=0.289). NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
Patients with treatment_sotorasib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_sotorasib == 0.
Patients with treatment_olaparib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_olaparib == 0.
Patients with treatment_osimertinib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_osimertinib == 0.

analyses.result_summary: treatment_pembrolizumab: treated mean PFS = 3.414 (n=25008) vs control = 3.449 (n=24992); diff = -0.035 mo (p=0.0811). NOT significant.
treatment_sotorasib: treated mean PFS = 3.641 (n=17544) vs control = 3.319 (n=32456); diff = 0.322 mo (p=5.62e-49). SIGNIFICANT positive.
treatment_olaparib: treated mean PFS = 3.414 (n=14999) vs control = 3.439 (n=35001); diff = -0.025 mo (p=0.256). NOT significant.
treatment_osimertinib: treated mean PFS = 3.434 (n=14976) vs control = 3.431 (n=35024); diff = 0.004 mo (p=0.87). NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is larger (more positive) in patients with higher pdl1_tps — i.e., positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction.
Within the subgroup pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta = -0.1164 (p=0.217). NOT significant — no evidence of pdl1-modified pembro effect. Main effect of pembro also null (beta=0.0009, p=0.981).
PD-L1 high (>=0.5): pembro diff = -0.070 mo (p=0.106). Direction is NEGATIVE (pembro slightly worse than control), opposite of the hypothesis. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with tmb_high == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction.
Within the subgroup tmb_high == 1, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0179 (p=0.685). NOT significant.
TMB high: pembro diff = -0.0480 mo (p=0.2). NOT significant; direction negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is smaller (more negative) in patients with stk11_mutation == 1 — i.e., negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction.
Within the subgroup stk11_mutation == 0 (STK11 wild-type), treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0162 (p=0.773). NOT significant.
STK11 wt: pembro diff = -0.0324 mo (p=0.136). Direction negative; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is much larger in patients with kras_g12c == 1 — i.e., strong positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction.
Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1, treatment_sotorasib == 1 patients have substantially longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0 patients.
Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 0, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = 2.5577 months (p=0). HIGHLY SIGNIFICANT — sotorasib effect is ~2.56 months larger in KRAS G12C+ patients.
KRAS G12C+: sotorasib mean PFS = 5.766 vs control = 3.213; diff = +2.552 mo (p=1.66e-202). LARGE significant benefit, n_treated=2246.
KRAS G12C-: sotorasib diff = -0.0054 mo (p=0.799). Effectively zero — confirms targeted nature of effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with brca2_mutation == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction.
Within the subgroup brca2_mutation == 1, treatment_olaparib == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_olaparib == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = 0.0367 (p=0.778). NOT significant — no evidence of brca2-modified olaparib effect, contrary to expectation.
BRCA2+: olaparib diff = 0.0104 mo (p=0.934). NOT significant. n_treated=417, n_control=1102.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with egfr_mutation == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction.
Within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1, treatment_osimertinib == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0199 (p=0.758). NOT significant — no evidence that osimertinib differentially benefits EGFR+, contrary to clinical expectation.
EGFR+: osimertinib diff = -0.0141 mo (p=0.808). NOT significant. n_treated=1973, n_control=4544.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all clinical features (age, sex, smoking, ECOG, histology, stage, brain mets, mutations, biomarkers, labs) explains a large fraction of variance (R² > 0.5).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable clinical model: R² = 0.8892, n = 50000. ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, smoking, albumin, weight loss, histology, ALK fusion, KRAS dominate. Confirms strong, predictable signal in clinical variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding treatments and the named treatment×biomarker interactions (pembro×PDL1, pembro×TMB, pembro×STK11, sotorasib×KRAS, olaparib×BRCA2, osimertinib×EGFR, osimertinib×brain_mets) to the multivariable model meaningfully increases R² beyond the clinical-only model.
After full adjustment, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction remains the dominant statistically significant treatment-biomarker term.

analyses.result_summary: Full model R² = 0.9267 vs clinical-only R² = 0.8892. Increase of ~0.037 is driven primarily by the sotorasib×KRAS interaction.

analyses.code: extract treatment-related coefficients from full model
analyses.result_summary: In the adjusted full model: sotorasib:kras_g12c beta=2.535 (p=0) — only significant treatment term. All other treatment main effects and interactions (pembro:pdl1, pembro:tmb, pembro:stk11, olaparib:brca2, osimertinib:egfr, osimertinib:brain_mets) have p > 0.05 in adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all candidate modifiers (sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, pdl1_high, smoking_status, histology), there exists at least one with a significant pembrolizumab × modifier interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for mod in modifiers: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * {mod}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Heterogeneity scan: only marginal hits. Most notable: pembro × stage_iv beta=+0.108 (p=0.00677); pembro × kras_g12c beta=-0.113 (p=0.0565); pembro × egfr beta=0.099 (p=0.0964). Only stage_iv passes p<0.05 nominally; none survive multiplicity correction across ~14 tests. No robust heterogeneity for pembrolizumab.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond kras_g12c, additional modifiers (e.g., sex_female, smoking_status, histology, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, egfr_mutation) significantly modify the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months.
Specifically, the treatment_sotorasib effect is smaller in sex_female == 1 patients (negative treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction).

analyses.code: for mod in modifiers: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * {mod}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Strong interactions: sotorasib × kras_g12c beta=+2.558 (p=0, dominant); sotorasib × sex_female beta=-0.562 (p=5.3e-41); sotorasib × smoking[never] beta=-0.497 (p=3.15e-14); sotorasib × alk_fusion beta=-0.488 (p=4.13e-07); sotorasib × egfr beta=-0.241 (p=9.93e-05). Most of these reflect the marginal mutation distribution: KRAS G12C is far more frequent in former/current smokers and squamous-less, and is mutually exclusive with EGFR/ALK — so removing those subgroups removes the KRAS-driven effect.
sotorasib:sex_female interaction beta = -0.562 (p=5.3e-41). Significant negative — sotorasib effect is smaller in females. Likely reflects lower KRAS G12C prevalence in females.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all candidate modifiers, there exists at least one with a significant treatment_olaparib × modifier interaction on pfs_months (including but not limited to brca2_mutation).

analyses.code: for mod in modifiers: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib * {mod}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All olaparib × modifier interactions are non-significant. Smallest p: olaparib × stk11_mutation (term treatment_olaparib:stk11_mutation) beta=0.110 (p=0.0728). No subgroup shows benefit; olaparib appears inert in this dataset across every modifier tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all candidate modifiers (including egfr_mutation, has_brain_mets, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation), there exists at least one with a significant treatment_osimertinib × modifier interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for mod in modifiers: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ treatment_osimertinib * {mod}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Two nominally significant hits: osimertinib × alk_fusion beta=0.238 (p=0.0172); osimertinib × brca2_mutation beta=-0.254 (p=0.0496). Neither survives multiplicity correction; both involve small subgroups. Crucially, osimertinib × egfr_mutation is NOT significant (beta=-0.020, p=0.758), contrary to the canonical clinical assumption.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the compound subgroup defined by pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND tmb_high == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
The 3-way interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high × stk11_mutation is significant within the pdl1_high subgroup.
Within the subgroup pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.

analyses.result_summary: PDL1-hi & TMB-hi & STK11-wt: pembro diff = +0.056 mo (p=0.522); n_treated=1373, n_control=1341. Direction positive but NOT significant — the 'classical' best-case immunotherapy biomarker stack does not yield a real benefit here.

Within PD-L1 high: pembro main beta=-0.156 (p=0.00531); pembro:tmb_high beta=0.212 (p=0.0395); pembro:stk11 beta=0.167 (p=0.244); 3-way beta=0.025 (p=0.926). 3-way NOT significant; pembro×tmb_high marginally positive within PD-L1 high subgroup.
PDL1-hi & STK11-wt: pembro diff = -0.0953 mo (p=0.0436). Direction NEGATIVE; nominally significant in the wrong direction. n_treated=4552.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1 AND has_brain_mets == 1, treatment_osimertinib == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib == 0 (osimertinib is CNS-penetrant).
The 3-way interaction treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation × has_brain_mets on pfs_months is significant.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(pfs[osi=1 & egfr=1 & brain=1], pfs[osi=0 & egfr=1 & brain=1])
analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ & brain-mets+: osimertinib diff = +0.051 mo (p=0.639). NOT significant; n_treated=503, n_control=1132.

3-way model: osimertinib main beta=-0.003 (p=0.913); osi:egfr beta=-0.026 (p=0.721); osi:brain beta=0.025 (p=0.644); 3-way beta=0.056 (p=0.703). All non-significant. No osimertinib effect, regardless of EGFR/brain-met combination.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_sotorasib == 1 produces substantially longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0.

Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 1, the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is smaller than in the kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0 subgroup (negative 3-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × stk11_mutation).

analyses.code: ttest_ind(pfs[sotorasib=1 & kras=1 & stk11=0], pfs[sotorasib=0 & kras=1 & stk11=0])
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+ & STK11-wt: sotorasib mean PFS = 5.789 vs control = 3.202; diff = +2.587 mo (p=3.22e-179); n_treated=1959. LARGEST observed treatment effect in the dataset.

3-way model: sotorasib:kras_g12c beta=2.594 (p=0); 3-way sotorasib:kras:stk11 beta=-0.271 (p=0.128). 3-way is negative (suggesting somewhat smaller benefit when STK11 mutated) but NOT significant (p≈0.13). Subgroup-level: KRAS+/STK11mut still shows large benefit (diff = +2.326 mo, p=3.6e-25, n_treated=287). STK11 status is NOT a meaningful suppressor of sotorasib effect in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months remains positive and significant across all three ecog_ps strata (0, 1, 2).
The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs across ecog_ps strata; specifically, pembrolizumab is harmful (negative diff) in ecog_ps == 1.
treatment_olaparib and treatment_osimertinib have null effects on pfs_months in every ecog_ps stratum.

analyses.code: for ecog in [0,1,2]: ttest_ind(pfs[sotorasib & ecog==e], pfs[control & ecog==e])
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: sotorasib diff=+0.313 mo (p=6.38e-18); ECOG 1: +0.314 (p=7.11e-27); ECOG 2: +0.296 (p=2.19e-09). All three significant — sotorasib effect persists across performance status.

analyses.code: for ecog in [0,1,2]: ttest_ind(pfs[pembro & ecog==e], pfs[control & ecog==e])
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: pembro diff=0.0311 (p=0.346); ECOG 1: -0.0663 (p=0.0125); ECOG 2: -0.0101 (p=0.819). ECOG 1 has small significant negative diff (p<0.05); other strata null. Likely chance after multiple comparisons; pembro effect remains essentially null overall.

analyses.code: for trt in [olaparib, osimertinib]: for ecog in [0,1,2]: ttest_ind(...)
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib (ECOG 0/1/2): diff=-0.0012/-0.0071/-0.0698 (p=0.97/0.81/0.15). Osimertinib (ECOG 0/1/2): diff=0.0124/0.0125/-0.0299 (p=0.73/0.67/0.53). All null — confirms no PFS benefit from olaparib or osimertinib at any performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the smoking_status == 'never' subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have similar pfs_months to treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients (no benefit in never-smokers).
Within the smoking_status == 'former' subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients (largest expected benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Never-smokers: pembro diff = -0.0081 mo (p=0.87). NOT significant — null effect.
Former smokers: pembro diff = -0.0475 mo (p=0.0773). NOT significant; direction NEGATIVE. Current smokers: diff = -0.0287 (p=0.432). No subgroup of smoking status shows a pembro PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the sex_female == 0 (male) subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
Within the sex_female == 1 (female) subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Males: pembro diff = -0.0438 mo (p=0.119). NOT significant; direction negative.
Females: pembro diff = -0.0237 mo (p=0.399). NOT significant; direction negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even using a 3-way interaction with histology (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × histology), the treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months remains null in every BRCA2/histology subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way model with histology: no significant olaparib-related coefficients (all p > 0.1). Confirms olaparib is inert across BRCA2 status and histology in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL — Pembrolizumab: there is NO subgroup of pdl1_tps, tmb_high, or stk11_mutation in which treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces a longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0; the most-studied compound subgroup (pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND tmb_high == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0) shows a non-significant difference.

FINAL — Sotorasib: within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1, treatment_sotorasib == 1 produces a markedly longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0; the effect is preserved when further restricting to kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0 (no STK11 suppression).
FINAL — Olaparib: within the subgroup brca2_mutation == 1, treatment_olaparib has no effect on pfs_months; the named biomarker hypothesis is refuted in this dataset.
FINAL — Osimertinib: within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1, treatment_osimertinib has no effect on pfs_months; the named biomarker hypothesis is refuted in this dataset.

analyses.result_summary: PDL1-hi & TMB-hi & STK11-wt: pembro diff = +0.056 mo (p=0.522); n_treated=1373. Direction positive but NOT significant. Cannot identify any subgroup where pembrolizumab confers a real PFS benefit in this dataset.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(pfs[sotorasib=1 & kras=1], pfs[sotorasib=0 & kras=1]) AND further restricted
analyses.result_summary: KRAS G12C+: sotorasib diff = +2.552 mo (p=1.66e-202); n_treated=2246. KRAS G12C+ AND STK11 wt (strict): diff = +2.587 mo (p=3.22e-179); n_treated=1959. Effect is large, robust, and confined to KRAS G12C+ patients. STK11 mutation does NOT meaningfully suppress sotorasib here (KRAS+/STK11mut still shows ~+2.3 mo benefit).
BRCA2+: olaparib diff = +0.0104 mo (p=0.934); n_treated=417. NOT significant. Hypothesis refuted.
EGFR+: osimertinib diff = -0.0141 mo (p=0.808); n_treated=1973. NOT significant. Hypothesis refuted.
